blocks.body: A number between 0 and 1 (or 0% and 100%) that measures how likely an event is to occur. P = 0 means impossible, P = 1 means certain.
Theoretical Probability: P(event) = (number of favorable outcomes) / (total number of equally likely outcomes). Example: P(rolling a 3 on a die) = 1/6.
Experimental Probability: P(event) = (number of times event occurred) / (total number of trials). Based on actual results rather than theory.
The complement of event A is "not A" — all outcomes that are not A. P(not A) = 1 - P(A). Example: P(not rolling a 3) = 1 - 1/6 = 5/6.

blocks.label: Example
blocks.body: A bag has 3 red, 5 blue, 2 green marbles. P(blue) = 5/10 = 1/2. P(not blue) = 1 - 1/2 = 1/2. P(red or green) = 5/10 = 1/2.
All probabilities in a sample space sum to 1. The sample space is the set of all possible outcomes.
